mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS levels across rolling 3-year windows, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 5.0% signals labor market deterioration and impending economic contraction, triggering immediate risk-off selling that depresses S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE crosses above 5.0%, or the signal loses predictive power during non-recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate revenue growth expectations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when either series declines, or the interaction term shows negative out-of-sample IC over a 2-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side resilience, which supports corporate earnings growth and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns during expansionary phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to precede positive 20-day SPX returns during early-cycle periods, or the mechanism reverses direction when inflation exceeds 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid monthly inflation acceleration above 0.5% forces markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns remain positive in over 60% of months where CPIAUCSL rises by more than 0.5%, or the signal shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market health, boosting consumer spending capacity and driving positive S&P 500 returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal produces negative 60-day SPX returns in more than half of observed instances, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for monetary policy shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth reflects expanding economic output and corporate profitability, which directly translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero or negative correlation with 20-day SPX forward returns across multiple rolling windows, or Rank IC consistently remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a high-inflation environment signals supply chain bottlenecks and stagflation risks, triggering immediate equity sell-offs that depress S&P 500 returns over five trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5-day returns in over 50% of instances when INDPRO declines during high-inflation periods, or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold baseline in walk-forward tests.
